rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative [3+2] cycloaddition of styrenes with 4-N-Boc-aminophenols to construct 5-aminocoumarans (2,3-dihydrobenzofurans) in an undivided cell
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of 4-N-Boc-aminophenol generates a phenoxyl/aryl-N radical (delocalized); cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: Delocalized carbon-centered (ortho) radical / quinone-imine-like radical II
Benzylic radical adduct III after addition to styrene 1a
Benzylic carbocation IV after second SET oxidation

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes 4-N-Boc-aminophenol (2a) by SET (with H+ loss) to a phenoxyl radical I that delocalizes to ortho-C radical II; II adds to styrene 1a to give benzylic radical III, which undergoes a second anodic SET to cation IV; intramolecular O-attack closes the dihydrobenzofuran (loss of H+), while H+ is reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully inhibited; HRMS adducts 4a/4b corresponding to intermediates II and III detected

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully inhibited; HRMS adducts 5a/5b corresponding to intermediates II and III detected

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Onset oxidation potentials ~+1.15 V (1a) vs ~+0.55 V (2a); 2a is preferentially oxidized at the anode

notes: Unsuccessful substrates (electron-poor vinylarenes 1A-1D, 2-halo aminophenols 2A/2B, N-unprotected/NMe2/NHAc aminophenols 2C-2E, and p-methoxyphenol 2F) support the requirement for an easily oxidized N-protected aminophenol that generates a stabilized delocalized radical. No catalyst, oxidant, or sacrificial reagent needed; H2 is the only byproduct.
